content_sections.VI Results and Discussion VI.2 Finite Wi: The finite-width effect is accounted for by the quantity ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR defined by citebib.bib82, bib.bib83 ηR≡Γ​(D→R​P3→P1​P2​P3)ΓR→0Γ​(D→R​P3→P1​P2​P3)=Γ​(D→R​P3)​ℬ​(R→P1​P2)Γ​(D→R​P3→P1​P2​P3)=1+δ,subscript𝜂𝑅Γsubscript→𝐷𝑅subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃3→subscriptΓ𝑅0Γ→𝐷𝑅subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃3Γ→𝐷𝑅subscript𝑃3ℬ→𝑅subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2Γ→𝐷𝑅subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃31𝛿displaystyleetaRequivfracGamma(Dto RP3to P1P2P3)GammaRto 0Gamma(Dto RP3to P1P2P3)=fracGamma(Dto RP3)cal B(Rto P1P2)Gamma(Dto RP3to P1P2P3)=1+delta, so that the deviation of ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR from unity measures the degree of departure from the NWA when the resonance width is finite. It is naïvely expected that the correction δ𝛿delta will be of order ΓR/mRsubscriptΓ𝑅subscript𝑚𝑅GammaR/mR. It is calculable theoretically but depends on the line shape of the resonance and the approach of describing weak hadronic decays such as QCD factorization and perturbative QCD. Using the branching fractions of two-body and three-body D𝐷D decays calculated in Tables and , respectively, in scheme II, the resultant ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR parameters for scalar resonances σ,κ𝜎𝜅sigma,kappa and K0∗superscriptsubscript𝐾0K0* produced in the three-body D𝐷D decays are summarized in Table. We only consider the D+superscript𝐷D+ decays as the three-body modes listed in Table are not contaminated by the W𝑊W-annihilation amplitude and hence the calculated finite width effects are more trustworth. We have also checked explicitly that ηR→1→subscript𝜂𝑅1etaRto 1 in the narrow width limit as it should be. The ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR parameters for various resonances produced in the three-body B𝐵B decays have been evaluated in citebib.bib82, bib.bib83. Our results for ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR’s in Table have similar features as the values ησ/f0​=2.15±0.05subscript𝜂𝜎subscript𝑓0500plus-or-minus2.150.05etasigma/f0=2.15pm 0.05 and ηK0∗​=0.83±0.04subscript𝜂superscriptsubscript𝐾01430plus-or-minus0.830.04etaK0*=0.83pm 0.04 obtained in B𝐵B decays. Note that a priori we do not know if the deviation of ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR from unity is positive or negative. In general, it depends on the line shape, mass and width of the resonance. As alluded to above, the mass and width have a more dominant effect than the line shape in the case of κ​𝜅700kappa. As another example, we found in Ref. that ηρ>1subscript𝜂𝜌1etarho>1 for the Breit-Wigner line shape and ηρ<1subscript𝜂𝜌1etarho<1 when the Gounaris-Sakurai model citebib.bib84 is used to describe the line shape of the broad ρ​𝜌770rho resonance. To our knowledge, there is no good argument favoring one line shape over the other. Therefore, ηK0∗​=0.985<1subscript𝜂superscriptsubscript𝐾014300.9851etaK0*=0.985<1, for example, is the result of our particular line shape choice. When the resonance is sufficiently broad, it is necessary to take into account the finite-width effects characterized by the parameter ηRsubscript𝜂𝑅etaR. Explicitly citebib.bib82, bib.bib83, ℬ​(D→R​P)=ηR​ℬ​(D→R​P)NWA=ηR​ℬ​(D→R​P3→P1​P2​P3)exptℬ​(R→P1​P2)expt,ℬ→𝐷𝑅𝑃subscript𝜂𝑅ℬsubscript→𝐷𝑅𝑃NWAsubscript𝜂𝑅ℬsubscript→𝐷𝑅subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃3exptℬsubscript→𝑅subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2exptdisplaystylecal B(Dto RP)=etaRcal B(Dto RP)rm NWA=etaRcal B(Dto RP3to P1P2P3)rm exptovercal B(Rto P1P2)rm expt, Therefore, the experimental branching fractions ℬ​(D→R​P)NWAℬsubscript→𝐷𝑅𝑃NWAcal B(Dto RP)rm NWA for D+→σ​π+,κ¯0​π+→superscript𝐷𝜎superscript𝜋superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋D+tosigmapi+,barkappa0pi+ and K¯0∗0​π+superscriptsubscript¯𝐾0absent0superscript𝜋overlineK0*0pi+ decays in Tables and should have the following corrections: ℬ​(D+→σ​π+)::ℬ→superscript𝐷𝜎superscript𝜋absentdisplaystylecal B(D+tosigmapi+): (2.1±0.2)×10−3→(3.8±0.3)×10−3,→plus-or-minus2.10.2superscript103plus-or-minus3.80.3superscript103displaystyle(2.1pm 0.2)times 10-3to(3.8pm 0.3)times 10-3, ℬ​(D+→κ¯0​π+)::ℬ→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋absentdisplaystylecal B(D+tobarkappa0pi+): (3.6−2.4+3.0)%→(6.7−4.5+5.6)%,displaystyle(3.6+3.0-2.4)%qquadquadto(6.7+5.6-4.5)%, ℬ​(D+→K¯0∗0​π+)::ℬ→superscript𝐷superscriptsubscript¯𝐾0absent0superscript𝜋absentdisplaystylecal B(D+tooverlineK0*0pi+): (1.98±0.22)%→(1.94±0.22)%. displaystyle(1.98pm 0.22)%quadto(1.94pm 0.22)%. From Table , it is evident that the agreement between theory and experiment is substantially improved for D+→σ​π+→superscript𝐷𝜎superscript𝜋D+tosigmapi+ and D+→κ¯0​π+→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋D+tobarkappa0pi+. If we employ the pole mass and width, mκ=648±7subscript𝑚𝜅plus-or-minus6487mkappa=648pm 7 MeV and Γκ=560±32subscriptΓ𝜅plus-or-minus56032Gammakappa=560pm 32 MeV, respectively, for κ/K0∗​𝜅superscriptsubscript𝐾0700kappa/K0* and the pole line shape given in Eq. , we will be led to the results ℬ​(D+→κ¯0​π+)=8.10%ℬ→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋percent8.10cal B(D+tobarkappa0pi+)=8.10%, ℬ​(D+→κ¯0​π+→KS0​π0​π+)=1.62×10−3ℬ→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋→superscriptsubscript𝐾𝑆0superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋1.62superscript103cal B(D+tobarkappa0pi+to KS0pi0pi+)=1.62times 10-3 and ηκ=8.34subscript𝜂𝜅8.34etakappa=8.34. This implies that the finite-width correction will be unreasonably too large and thus unlikely, as alluded to at the end of Sec. . However, if the Breit-Wigner mass and width are used instead, we get ηκ=1.92subscript𝜂𝜅1.92etakappa=1.92 for pole line shape, which is a more reasonable result. This implies that in this case, it is the mass and width rather than the line shape that governs the finite-width correction. For the case of f0​subscript𝑓0500f0, one may wonder what the correction will be if the Breit-Wigner line shape is used. According to PDG citebib.bib1, the Breit-Wigner mass and width of f0​subscript𝑓0500f0 lie in the wide ranges of 400-800MeV and 100-800MeV, respectively. As a result, it is quite difficult to pin down a specific set of parameters and thereby determine the finite-width correction. On the contrary, LHCb has determined its pole mass and width with reasonable accuracy using the pole line shape [see Eq. ]. It turns out that the pole mass and width fall within the above allowed ranges of the Breit-Wigner mass and width. Therefore, it is more sensible to use pole mass and width for calculations in either line shapes.
content_sections.VII Conclusions: In this work we have examined the quasi-two-body D→S​P→𝐷𝑆𝑃Dto SP decays and the three-body D𝐷D decays proceeding through intermediate scalar resonances. Our main results are: • In the D→S​P3→P1​P2​P3→𝐷𝑆subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃3Dto SP3to P1P2P3 decays, we cannot extract the two-body branching fractions ℬ​(D→S​P)ℬ→𝐷𝑆𝑃cal B(Dto SP) for S=f0​𝑆subscript𝑓0980S=f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 due to the lack of information of ℬ​(S→P1​P2)ℬ→𝑆subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2cal B(Sto P1P2) (except for a0​→π​η→subscript𝑎0980𝜋𝜂a0topieta). For S=κ/K0∗​𝑆𝜅superscriptsubscript𝐾0700S=kappa/K0* and σ/f0​𝜎subscript𝑓0500sigma/f0, the extracted two-body branching fractions are subject to large finite-width effects owing to their broad widths. Hence, for light scalars it is more sensible to study ℬ​(D→S​P→P1​P2​P)ℬ→𝐷𝑆𝑃→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2𝑃cal B(Dto SPto P1P2P) directly and compare with experiment. • We have considered the two-quark (scheme I) and four-quark (scheme II) descriptions of the light scalar mesons with masses below or close to 1 GeV. Recent BESIII measurements of semileptonic charm decays favor the SU nonet tetraquark description of the f0​subscript𝑓0500f0, f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 produced in charmed meson decay. In Table we have calculated D→S​P3→P1​P2​P3→𝐷𝑆subscript𝑃3→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2subscript𝑃3Dto SP3to P1P2P3 in schemes I and II. It is evident that scheme II agrees better with experiment for decays such as D+→f0​π+→superscript𝐷subscript𝑓0superscript𝜋D+to f0pi+ followed by f0→π+​π−→subscript𝑓0superscript𝜋superscript𝜋f0topi+pi- and D+→f0​K+→superscript𝐷subscript𝑓0superscript𝐾D+to f0K+ followed by f0→π+​π−→subscript𝑓0superscript𝜋superscript𝜋f0topi+pi- or f0→K+​K−→subscript𝑓0superscript𝐾superscript𝐾f0to K+K-. This again favors the tetraquark structure for light scalars. The predicted rates for D0→f0​P,a0​P→superscript𝐷0subscript𝑓0𝑃subscript𝑎0𝑃D0to f0P,a0P are generally smaller than experimental data by one order of magnitude, presumably implying the importance of W𝑊W-exchange. • The three-body decay modes D+→κ¯0(→KSπ0)π+D+tobarkappa0(to KSpi0)pi+, D+→K¯0∗(→K−π+)π+D+tooverlineK0*(to K-pi+)pi+ and D+→K¯0∗(→KSπ0)π+D+tooverlineK0*(to KSpi0)pi+ are ideal for testing the validity of the factorization approach as they are free of W𝑊W-annihilation contributions. T𝑇T and C′superscript𝐶′Cprime amplitudes contribute constructively, contrary to the Cabibbo-allowed D+→K¯0​π+→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝐾0superscript𝜋D+tooverlineK0pi+ decay where the interference between external and internal W𝑊W-emission is destructive. • Denoting the primed amplitudes T′superscript𝑇′Tprime and C′superscript𝐶′Cprime for the case when the emitted meson is a scalar meson, it is naïvely expected that T′=C′=0superscript𝑇′superscript𝐶′0Tprime=Cprime=0 for the neutral scalars σ,f0𝜎subscript𝑓0sigma,f0 and a00superscriptsubscript𝑎00a00, |T′|≪|T|much-less-thansuperscript𝑇′𝑇|Tprime|ll|T| and |C′|≪|C|much-less-thansuperscript𝐶′𝐶|Cprime|ll|C| for the charged a0subscript𝑎0a0 and |T′|<|T|superscript𝑇′𝑇|Tprime|<|T| and |C′|<|C|superscript𝐶′𝐶|Cprime|<|C| for the κ𝜅kappa and K0∗​superscriptsubscript𝐾01430K0*. Beyond the factorization approximation, contributions proportional to the scalar decay constant f¯Ssubscript¯𝑓𝑆barfS can be produced from vertex and hard spectator-scattering corrections for the above-mentioned neutral scalars. • We have studied the flavor operators a1,2​(M1​M2)subscript𝑎12subscript𝑀1subscript𝑀2a1,2(M1M2) for M1​M2=S​Psubscript𝑀1subscript𝑀2𝑆𝑃M1M2=SP and P​S𝑃𝑆PS within the framework of QCD factorization. Notice that ai​(P​S)subscript𝑎𝑖𝑃𝑆ai(PS) and ai​(S​P)subscript𝑎𝑖𝑆𝑃ai(SP) are very different as the former does not receive factorizable contributions. While a1,2​(S​P)subscript𝑎12𝑆𝑃a1,2(SP) are similar for any light and heavy scalar mesons, a1​(P​S)subscript𝑎1𝑃𝑆a1(PS) and a2​(P​S)subscript𝑎2𝑃𝑆a2(PS) vary from neutral to the charged ones as shown in Table . The flavor operators a1,2​(π​a0±)subscript𝑎12𝜋superscriptsubscript𝑎0plus-or-minusa1,2(pi a0pm) are much greater than a1,2​(π​a00)subscript𝑎12𝜋superscriptsubscript𝑎00a1,2(pi a00). In general, a1,2​(P​S)subscript𝑎12𝑃𝑆a1,2(PS) become larger when the vector decay constants become smaller. • For f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0, we use the Flatté line shape to describe both of them to take into account the threshold and coupled channel effects. For the very broad σ/f0​𝜎subscript𝑓0500sigma/f0 , we follow LHCb to employ a simple pole description. • The annihilation amplitude inferred from the measurement of Ds+→a0+,0​π0,+→η​π+,0​π0,+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋0→𝜂superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋0Ds+to a0+,0pi0,+toetapi+,0pi0,+ is given by |A|=(0.91±0.12)×10−6​GeV𝐴plus-or-minus0.910.12superscript106GeV|A|=(0.91pm 0.12)times 10-6,rm GeV. It is very sizable in the S​P𝑆𝑃SP sector, |A/T|S​P∼1/2similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑆𝑃12|A/T|SPsim 1/2, contrary to its suppression in the P​P𝑃𝑃P!P sector with |A/T|P​P∼0.18similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑃𝑃0.18|A/T|PPsim 0.18. • Since σ𝜎sigma and κ𝜅kappa are very broad, we have considered their finite-width effects characterized by the parameter ηSsubscript𝜂𝑆etaS, whose deviation from unity measures the degree of departure from the NWA when the resonance width is finite. We find ησsubscript𝜂𝜎etasigma and ηκsubscript𝜂𝜅etakappa to be of order 1.85−1.871.851.871.85-1.87. The experimental branching fractions ℬ​(D+→σ​π+)ℬ→superscript𝐷𝜎superscript𝜋cal B(D+tosigmapi+) and ℬ​(D+→κ¯0​π+)ℬ→superscript𝐷superscript¯𝜅0superscript𝜋cal B(D+tobarkappa0pi+) should then read (3.8±0.3)×10−3plus-or-minus3.80.3superscript103(3.8pm 0.3)times 10-3 and (6.7−4.5+5.6)%percentsubscriptsuperscript6.75.64.5(6.7+5.6-4.5)%, respectively. • For each scalar nonet (lighter and heavier one) we have 15 unknown parameters for the 8 topological amplitudes T,C,E,A𝑇𝐶𝐸𝐴T,C,E,A and T′,C′,E′,A′superscript𝑇′superscript𝐶′superscript𝐸′superscript𝐴′Tprime,Cprime,Eprime,Aprime. However, there are only 14 independent data to fit. Moreover, since we need to introduce appropriate energy-dependent line shapes for the scalar mesons, it is not conceivable to extract the topological amplitudes from three-body decays as the decay rates cannot be factorized into the topological amplitude squared and the phase space factor.

sorted_papers.title: Tetraquark nature of the $a_0(980)$ meson in hadronic $D$ decays
sorted_papers.abstract:
The internal structure of the light scalar meson $a_0(980)$ is explored in
the three-body $D$ decays of $D\to a_0(980)P\to P_1P_2P$ through the
intermediate state $a_0(980)$, where $P$ denotes a pseudoscalar meson. The
quasi-two-body $D\to a_0(980)^+P$ decays are governed by the external
$W$-emission diagram in which $a_0(980)^+$ is emitted. The predicted branching
fractions in the $q\bar q$ model of $a_0(980)$ are too small by one to two
orders of magnitude compared to experiment as the amplitude is suppressed by
the smallness of the $a_0(980)^+$ decay constant, while those for $D^+\to
a_0(980)^0 P$ and $D^0\to a_0(980)^{-}P$ are usually too large. These
discrepancies can be resolved provided that $a_0(980)$ is a tetraquark state.
In this case, there exist two additional $T$-like topological amplitudes,
denoted by $\overline{T}$ and $\tilde T$ which readily account for the
discrepancies. An important implication of the tetraquark model is that the
$D_s^+\to a_0(980)^+\pi^0+a_0(980)^0\pi^+$ decay is not a purely
$W$-annihilation process as in the diquark model of $a_0(980)$; it receives
dominant contributions from $\overline{T}$ newly noticed in this work.
Therefore, measurements of $(D,D_s^+)\to a_0(980)P$ decays lend strong support
to the tetraquark picture of $a_0(980)$.

qualitative: The internal structure of the $a_0(980)$ meson is investigated in the context of three-body $D$ decays, particularly $D \to a_0(980)P \to P_1P_2P$, where $a_0(980)$ acts as an intermediate state.

In the $q\bar{q}$ quark model, the $a_0(980)$ meson has a small decay constant, leading to a suppression of the $W$-emission amplitude in $D \to a_0(980)^+P$ decays, resulting in predicted branching fractions that are too small by one to two orders of magnitude compared to experimental observations.
For $D^+ \to a_0(980)^0 P$ and $D^0 \to a_0(980)^- P$ decays, the $q\bar{q}$ model predicts branching fractions that are too large, indicating a significant discrepancy with experiment.

These discrepancies are resolved if the $a_0(980)$ is interpreted as a tetraquark state, which introduces two additional topological amplitudes, $\overline{T}$ and $\tilde{T}$, not present in the $q\bar{q}$ model.
The $\overline{T}$ and $\tilde{T}$ amplitudes are $T$-like and arise due to the tetraquark structure, enabling a more consistent description of the observed decay rates.

The decay $D_s^+ \to a_0(980)^+\pi^0 + a_0(980)^0\pi^+$ is not purely a $W$-annihilation process as assumed in the diquark model; instead, it is dominated by the newly identified $\overline{T}$ amplitude in the tetraquark picture.
Measurements of $(D, D_s^+) \to a_0(980)P$ decays provide strong empirical support for the tetraquark nature of the $a_0(980)$ meson.
quantitative: Predicted branching fractions in the $q\bar{q}$ model are too small by one to two orders of magnitude compared to experiment for $D \to a_0(980)^+P$ decays.
Branching fractions for $D^+ \to a_0(980)^0 P$ and $D^0 \to a_0(980)^- P$ in the $q\bar{q}$ model are too large compared to experimental data.
The presence of two additional topological amplitudes $\overline{T}$ and $\tilde{T}$ in the tetraquark model accounts for the observed discrepancies in branching fractions.
The decay $D_s^+ \to a_0(980)^+\pi^0 + a_0(980)^0\pi^+$ receives dominant contributions from the $\overline{T}$ amplitude, not from $W$-annihilation as in the diquark model.

sorted_papers.reason:
论文紧扣弱衰变因子化近似与拓扑振幅分析主题，明确讨论 $D$ 衰变中 $W$-发射与 $T$-型振幅，验证 $a_0(980)$ 的四夸克结构，与查询核心一致。  

约束项检查：  
- C1：研究弱衰变因子化近似 — 满足。证据：“quasi-two-body $D\to a_0(980)^+P$ decays are governed by the external $W$-emission diagram” 对比 “弱衰变因子化近似”  
- C2：分析拓扑振幅 — 满足。证据：“two additional $T$-like topological amplitudes, denoted by $\overline{T}$ and $\tilde T$” 对比 “拓扑振幅分析”  
- C3：聚焦 $D$ 衰变过程 — 满足。证据：“$D\to a_0(980)P\to P_1P_2P$” 对比 “弱衰变因子化近似和拓扑振幅分析”  

不相关性 / 风险点 / 缺口：  
- 无。论文完全覆盖查询核心要求，无偏离。

B(D0to a0+pi-;a0+topi+eta)=(0.55pm 0.05pm 0.07)times 10-3, cal B(D0to a0-pi+;a0-topi-eta)=(0.07pm 0.02pm 0.01)times 10-3, cal B(D+to a0+pi0;a0+topi+eta)=(0.95pm 0.12pm 0.05)times 10-3, cal B(D+to a00pi+;a00topi0eta)=(0.37pm 0.10pm 0.04)times 10-3,endsplit which lead to the ratios: r+⁣/−≡ℬ​(D0→a0​+​π−)ℬ​(D0→a0​−​π+)=7.5−0.8+2.5±1.7,r+⁣/0≡ℬ​(D+→a0​+​π0)ℬ​(D+→a0​0​π+)=2.6±0.6±0.3. B(D0to a0+pi-)overcal B(D0to a0-pi+)=7.5+2.5-0.8pm 1.7, r+/0equivcal B(D+to a0+pi0)overcal B(D+to a00pi+)=2.6pm 0.6pm 0.3. endsplit Naively, the ratios are expected to be much smaller than unity because the quasi-two-body decays D→a0​+​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript980𝑃Dto a0+P, governed by the external W𝑊W-emission diagram with a0​+subscript𝑎0superscript980a0+ being an emitted meson, are suppressed by the smallness of the decay constant of a0​+subscript𝑎0superscript980a0+. The predicted branching fractions of D→a0​+​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript980𝑃Dto a0+P in the q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq model of light scalar mesons are too small by one to two orders of magnitude compared to the experiment (for details, see Table below). On the contrary, the calculated rates of D+→a0​0​P→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript9800𝑃D+to a00P and D0→a0​−​P→superscript𝐷0subscript𝑎0superscript980𝑃D0to a0-P are usually too large compared to the data. For example, realistic calculations in Ref. citebib.bib2 indicate that r+⁣/−=0.045subscript𝑟absent0.045r+/-=0.045 which is too small by two orders of magnitude compared to the BESIII measurement. This strongly implies that the diquark scenario for a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 fails to explain the data. In the tetraquark model, we shall see that for the above-mentioned decays there exist two additional T𝑇T-like topological diagrams which we call T𝑇tildeT and T¯¯𝑇overlineT (the corresponding diagrams are shown in Fig. ). Unlike the external W𝑊W-emission diagram T′superscript𝑇′Tprime in which a0​+subscript𝑎0superscript980a0+ is the emitted meson, they are not suppressed by the smallness of the decay constant of the charged a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 meson. Although these amplitudes are non-factorizable and thus pose difficulty in perturbative calculations, their information can be extracted from the data. We shall see that these T𝑇T-like topologies will enable us to resolve the large discrepancy between theory and experiment occurring in the q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq model of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0. In the Ds+superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠Ds+ sector, the decay Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ followed by a0​+,0→π+,0​η→subscript𝑎0superscript9800superscript𝜋0𝜂a0+,0topi+,0eta has been observed by BESIII with a branching fraction of (1.46±0.27)%percentplus-or-minus1.460.27(1.46pm 0.27)%citebib.bib3. If a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 is a q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq state, this decay proceeds only through the W𝑊W-annihilation diagram. However, its branching fraction at a percent level is much larger than the other two W𝑊W-annihilation channels Ds+→ω​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜔superscript𝜋Ds+toomegapi+ and ρ0​π+superscript𝜌0superscript𝜋rho0pi+ whose branching fractions are (1.92±0.30)×10−3plus-or-minus1.920.30superscript103(1.92pm 0.30)times 10-3 and (1.9±1.2)×10−4plus-or-minus1.91.2superscript104(1.9pm 1.2)times 10-4, respectivelycitebib.bib4. Naïvely, these data would imply that the annihilation amplitude is mysteriously sizable in the S​P𝑆𝑃SP sector, |A/T|S​P∼1/2similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑆𝑃12|A/T|SPsim 1/2citebib.bib2, in stark contrast to the suppressed |A/T|P​P∼0.18similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑃𝑃0.18|A/T|PPsim 0.18 in the P​P𝑃𝑃P!P sector and |AV/TP|V​P∼0.07similar-tosubscriptsubscript𝐴𝑉subscript𝑇𝑃𝑉𝑃0.07|AV/TP|VPsim 0.07 in the V​P𝑉𝑃V!P sectorcitebib.bib5. This is a puzzle as a priori there is no reason to expect a large enhancement of the W𝑊W-annihilation amplitude in the D→S​P→𝐷𝑆𝑃Dto SP decays. We find that if a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 is a tetraquark state instead, Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ will receive a contribution from T¯¯𝑇overlineT and the predicted rate agrees with experiment even in the absence of the W𝑊W-annihilation amplitude. This has an important implication that Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ is not a purely W𝑊W-annihilation process and thus the aforementioned puzzle is readily resolved. Very recently, BESIII made an observation of Λc+→Λ​a0​+→Λ​π+​η→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐Λsubscript𝑎0superscript980→Λsuperscript𝜋𝜂Lambdac+toLambda a0+toLambdapi+eta with a branching fraction of (1.05±0.16±0.05±0.07)%percentplus-or-minus1.050.160.050.07(1.05pm 0.16pm 0.05pm 0.07)%citebib.bib6, which is unexpectedly large in the diquark model. The factorizable contribution is rather suppressed due to the tiny a0+superscriptsubscript𝑎0a0+ decay constant. An early model calculation based on factorization and the pole model gave a branching fraction of order 1.9×10−41.9superscript1041.9times 10-4citebib.bib7. It had been improved by one order of magnitude after including final-state rescattering, yielding the result of (1.7−1.0+2.8±0.3)×10−3plus-or-minussubscriptsuperscript1.72.81.00.3superscript103(1.7+2.8-1.0pm 0.3)times 10-3citebib.bib8. In the tetraquark model of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0, however, this decay also proceeds through the diagram T𝑇tildeT to be introduced below, which allows to reach the branching fraction at a percent level. The layout of the present paper is as follows. In Section, we discuss the diquark q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq and tetraquark pictures of the scalar nonet near or below 1GeV and the discrimination of the two scenarios in semileptonic decays of charmed mesons. We turn to hadronic D→a0​​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝑃Dto a0P decays in Section and emphasize the new topological diagrams unique to the four-quark nature of light scalars. We give the explicit expressions for the factorizable amplitudes and extract the new topologies from the available data. Section comes to our conclusions. A detailed calculation of the three-body decay rate is presented in the Appendix.
content_sections.II Physical properties of light scalar m: The underlying structure of light scalar mesons is not well established even until today. Scalar mesons with masses below or close to 1GeV include the isoscalars σ/f0​𝜎subscript𝑓0500sigma/f0, f0​subscript𝑓0980f0, the isodoublet κ/K0∗​𝜅superscriptsubscript𝐾0700kappa/K0* and the isovector a0​subscript𝑎0980a0. If these scalar meson states are identified as the conventional low-lying 0+superscript00+ q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq nonet, their flavor wave functions read σ=12​(u​u¯+d​d¯),f0=s​s¯,formulae-sequence𝜎12𝑢¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑subscript𝑓0𝑠¯𝑠displaystylesigma=1oversqrt2(ubaru+dbard),qquadqquadf0=sbars, a00=12​(u​u¯−d​d¯),a0+=u​d¯,a0−=d​u¯,formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝑎0012𝑢¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝑎0𝑢¯𝑑superscriptsubscript𝑎0𝑑¯𝑢displaystyle a00=1oversqrt2(ubaru-dbard),qquadqquad a0+=ubard,qquad a0-=dbaru, κ+=u​s¯,κ0=d​s¯,κ¯0=s​d¯,κ−=s​u¯,formulae-sequencesuperscript𝜅𝑢¯𝑠formulae-sequencesuperscript𝜅0𝑑¯𝑠formulae-sequencesuperscript¯𝜅0𝑠¯𝑑superscript𝜅𝑠¯𝑢displaystylekappa+=ubars,qquadkappa0=dbars,qquadbarkappa0=sbard,qquadkappa-=sbaru, where an ideal mixing for f0subscript𝑓0f0 and σ𝜎sigma is assumed here as f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 is the heaviest one and σ𝜎sigma the lightest one in the light scalar nonet. However, this simple picture encounters several serious difficulties. Two of them are (i) it is impossible to understand the mass degeneracy between f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0, which is the so-called “inverted spectrum problem,” and (ii) it is difficult to explain why σ𝜎sigma and κ𝜅kappa are much broader than f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 in width. It turns out that these issues can be readily resolved in the tetraquark scenario where the four-quark flavor wave functions of light scalar mesons are symbolically given bycitebib.bib9 σ=u​u¯​d​d¯,f0=12​(u​u¯+d​d¯)​s​s¯,formulae-sequence𝜎𝑢¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑subscript𝑓012𝑢¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑𝑠¯𝑠displaystylesigma=ubarudbard,qquadqquadqquadf0=frac1sqrt2(ubaru+dbard)sbars, a00=12​(u​u¯−d​d¯)​s​s¯,a0+=u​d¯​s​s¯,a0−=d​u¯​s​s¯,formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝑎0012𝑢¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑𝑠¯𝑠formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝑎0𝑢¯𝑑𝑠¯𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0𝑑¯𝑢𝑠¯𝑠displaystyle a00=frac1sqrt2(ubaru-dbard)sbars,qquad a0+=ubardsbars,qquad a0-=dbarusbars, κ+=u​s¯​d​d¯,κ0=d​s¯​u​u¯,κ¯0=s​d¯​u​u¯,κ−=s​u¯​d​d¯. formulae-sequencesuperscript𝜅𝑢¯𝑠𝑑¯𝑑formulae-sequencesuperscript𝜅0𝑑¯𝑠𝑢¯𝑢formulae-sequencesuperscript¯𝜅0𝑠¯𝑑𝑢¯𝑢superscript𝜅𝑠¯𝑢𝑑¯𝑑displaystylekappa+=ubarsdbard,qquadkappa0=dbarsubaru,qquadbarkappa0=sbardubaru,qquadkappa-=sbarudbard. This four-quark description explains naturally the inverted mass spectrum of the light nonet, especially the mass degeneracy between f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0, and accounts for the broad widths of σ𝜎sigma and κ𝜅kappa while f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 are narrow because of the suppressed phase space for their decays to the kaon pairs. In the naïve diquark model with ideal mixing for f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and σ​𝜎500sigma, f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 is purely an s​s¯𝑠¯𝑠sbars state, while σ​𝜎500sigma is an n​n¯𝑛¯𝑛nbarn state with n​n¯≡(u¯​u+d¯​d)/2𝑛¯𝑛¯𝑢𝑢¯𝑑𝑑2nbarnequiv(baruu+bardd)/sqrt2. However, there also exists some experimental evidence indicating that f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 is not a purely s​s¯𝑠¯𝑠sbars state. For example, the observation of Γ​(J/ψ→f0​ω)≈12​Γ​(J/ψ→f0​ϕ)Γ→𝐽𝜓subscript𝑓0𝜔12Γ→𝐽𝜓subscript𝑓0italic-ϕGamma(J/psito f0omega)approx1over 2Gamma(J/psito f0phi)citebib.bib4 clearly shows the existence of both non-strange and strange quark contents in f0​subscript𝑓0980f0. Therefore, isoscalars σ​𝜎500sigma and f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 must have a nontrivial mixing |f0​⟩=|s​s¯⟩​cos⁡θ+|n​n¯⟩​sin⁡θ,|σ​⟩=−|s​s¯⟩​sin⁡θ+|n​n¯⟩​cos⁡θ. formulae-sequenceketsubscript𝑓0980ket𝑠¯𝑠𝜃ket𝑛¯𝑛𝜃ket𝜎500ket𝑠¯𝑠𝜃ket𝑛¯𝑛𝜃displaystyle|f0=|sbarscostheta+|nbarnsintheta,qquad|sigma=-|sbarssintheta+|nbarncostheta. Various mixing angle measurements have been discussed in the literature and summarized in Refs. citebib.bib10, bib.bib11. A recent measurement of the upper limit on the branching fraction product ℬ​(B¯0→J/ψ​f0​)×ℬ​(f0​→π+​π−)ℬ→superscript¯𝐵0𝐽𝜓subscript𝑓0980ℬ→subscript𝑓0980superscript𝜋superscript𝜋cal B(overlineB0to J/psi f0)timescal B(f0topi+pi-) by LHCb leads to |θ|2.7𝑅2.7R>2.7 at 90% confidence level and hence favor the SU nonet tetraquark description of the f0​subscript𝑓0500f0, f0​subscript𝑓0980f0 and a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 produced in charmed meson decays. In the diquark model, the strange quark content of σ/f0​𝜎subscript𝑓0500sigma/f0 depends on the f0−σsubscript𝑓0𝜎f0-sigma mixing angle, which is of order 30∘superscript3030circ, while in the tetraquark scenario of σ𝜎sigma, its strange content is quite suppressed as the mixing angle defined in Eq. is close to π𝜋pi. As a result, the predicted branching fraction of Ds+→f0​​e+​νe→π+​π−​e+​νe→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠subscript𝑓0500superscript𝑒subscript𝜈𝑒→superscript𝜋superscript𝜋superscript𝑒subscript𝜈𝑒Ds+to f0e+nuetopi+pi-e+nue can be very different in the 2-quark and 4-quark models. content_sections.III Nonleptonic D→a0​(980)​P→P1​P2​P→𝐷su: We now turn to the hadronic decays of D→a0​​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝑃Dto a0P followed by a0​→P1​P2→subscript𝑎0980subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2a0to P1P2 which can be used to discriminate the internal structure of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0. A suitable framework is the topological diagrammatic approach which we are going to describe below.

A model-independent analysis of heavy meson decays can be carried out in the so-called topological diagram approachcitebib.bib20, bib.bib21, bib.bib19. For the purpose of discussing decay rates, it suffices to consider just the tree-type amplitudes: color-allowed tree amplitude T𝑇T, color-suppressed tree amplitude C𝐶C, W𝑊W-exchange amplitude E𝐸E, and W𝑊W-annihilation amplitude A𝐴A. Just as in D→V​P→𝐷𝑉𝑃Dto V!P decays, one generally has for the D→S​P→𝐷𝑆𝑃Dto SP decays two sets of distinct diagrams for each topology. For example, there are two external W𝑊W-emission and two internal W𝑊W-emission diagrams, depending on whether the spectator quark of the charmed meson stays in the pseudoscalar or scalar meson. Following the convention in Refs. citebib.bib22, bib.bib23, we shall denote the primed amplitudes T′superscript𝑇′Tprime and C′superscript𝐶′Cprime for the case when the emitted meson is a scalar one (see Fig. ). For the W𝑊W-exchange and W𝑊W-annihilation diagrams with the final state q1​q¯2subscript𝑞1subscript¯𝑞2q1barq2, the primed amplitude denotes that the even-parity meson contains the q1subscript𝑞1q1 quark. If the light scalar meson is a tetraquark state, there exist additional topological diagrams T𝑇tildeT, T¯¯𝑇overlineT and C𝐶tildeC as depicted in Fig. . It follows that the topology C𝐶tildeC is equivalent to T𝑇tildeT, and hence this redundant contribution will not be considered in this work. We shall see below that the diagrams T𝑇tildeT and T¯¯𝑇overlineT are crucial for understanding the D→a0​​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝑃Dto a0P decays, whose topological amplitude decompositions are listed in Table in both diquark and tetraquark models for the light scalar mesons. The topological diagram T𝑇tildeT has been noticed before in Refs. citebib.bib24, bib.bib25. However, we find that the new contribution T¯¯𝑇overlineT is comparable to T𝑇T in size and plays a more dominant role than T𝑇tildeT in these decays.

For D+→a0​​π→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝜋D+to a0pi decays, their topological amplitudes are A​(D+→a0​+​π0)=12​Vc​d∗​Vu​d​(T′+C−T+T¯−A+A′),A​(D+→a0​0​π+)=12​Vc​d∗​Vu​d​(T+C′+T−T¯+A−A′). formulae-sequence𝐴→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript980superscript𝜋012superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑐𝑑subscript𝑉𝑢𝑑superscript𝑇′𝐶𝑇¯𝑇𝐴superscript𝐴′𝐴→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript9800superscript𝜋12superscriptsubscript𝑉𝑐𝑑subscript𝑉𝑢𝑑𝑇superscript𝐶′𝑇¯𝑇𝐴superscript𝐴′displaystylebeginsplitA(D+to a0+pi0)=1oversqrt2Vcd*Vud(Tprime+C-tildeT+overlineT-A+Aprime), A(D+to a00pi+)=1oversqrt2Vcd*Vud(T+Cprime+tildeT-overlineT+A-Aprime). endsplit We see from Table that the calculated branching fraction of D+→a0​0​π+→η​π0​π+→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript9800superscript𝜋→𝜂superscript𝜋0superscript𝜋D+to a00pi+toetapi0pi+ from the contributions of T+C′𝑇superscript𝐶′T+Cprime alone is too large by a factor of ∼3similar-toabsent3sim 3. This implies that a destructive contribution from the combination (T−T¯)𝑇¯𝑇(tildeT-overlineT) is needed. Hence, this favors the solution of T=−0.25​T𝑇0.25𝑇tildeT=-0.25T and rules out the other one with T=1.2​T𝑇1.2𝑇tildeT=1.2T.

To summarize, using the measurements of D+→a0+(→π+η)K¯0D+to a0+(topi+eta)overlineK0, D0→a0−(→π−η)π+D0to a0-(topi-eta)pi+ and D0→a00(→π0η)K¯0D0to a00(topi0eta)overlineK0 as the input, we obtain T=−(0.22−0.02+0.03)​T,T¯=(1.00−0.17+0.15)​T,E′/T=(0.725​ei​180∘∼0.961​ei​164∘). formulae-sequence𝑇subscriptsuperscript0.220.030.02𝑇formulae-sequence¯𝑇subscriptsuperscript1.000.150.17𝑇superscript𝐸′𝑇similar-to0.725superscript𝑒𝑖superscript1800.961superscript𝑒𝑖superscript164displaystyletildeT=-(0.22+0.03-0.02)T,qquadoverlineT=(1.00+0.15-0.17)T,qquad Eprime/T=(0.725ei180circsim 0.961ei164circ). The calculated branching fractions of various D→a0​​P→P1​P2​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝑃→subscript𝑃1subscript𝑃2𝑃Dto a0Pto P1P2P decays in the diquark and tetraquark models of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 are shown in Table where we have used the value of LCSR(II) for the form factor F0D​a0superscriptsubscript𝐹0𝐷subscript𝑎0F0Da0 (see Table). We see that the calculated results are generally in good agreement with experiment except for D0→a0+(→K+K¯0)π−D0to a0+(to K+overlineK0)pi- and D0→a0−(→K−K0)π+D0to a0-(to K-K0)pi+ where the predicted branching fractions are too small by one order of magnitude. However, the current experimental measurements of these two modes have very large uncertainties and need to be improved in the future. As discussed in Section, if a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 is a q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq state, the Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ decay proceeds only through the W𝑊W-annihilation topology. However, its branching fraction observed at a percent level is much larger than the other two W𝑊W-annihilation channels Ds+→ω​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠𝜔superscript𝜋Ds+toomegapi+ and ρ0​π+superscript𝜌0superscript𝜋rho0pi+. Hence, the question is why the annihilation amplitude is so sizable in the S​P𝑆𝑃SP sector with |A/T|S​P∼1/2similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑆𝑃12|A/T|SPsim 1/2citebib.bib2, whereas it is rather suppressed in the P​P𝑃𝑃P!P and V​P𝑉𝑃V!P sector with |A/T|P​P∼0.18similar-tosubscript𝐴𝑇𝑃𝑃0.18|A/T|PPsim 0.18 and |AV/TP|V​P∼0.07similar-tosubscriptsubscript𝐴𝑉subscript𝑇𝑃𝑉𝑃0.07|AV/TP|VPsim 0.07citebib.bib5. Although potentially large long-distance contributions to the W𝑊W-annihilation topology through the triangle diagrams at the hadron level had been evaluated in the literaturecitebib.bib35, bib.bib36, the aforementioned question was not really addressed. If a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 is a tetraquark state, the decay Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ receives an additional contribution of T¯¯𝑇overlineT. This contribution alone is slightly larger than the observed branching fraction (see Table) and the destructive contribution from W𝑊W-annihilation will help explain the data. We thus conclude that Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ is not a purely W𝑊W-annihilation process and that the W𝑊W-annihilation process in the S​P𝑆𝑃SP sector is not greatly enhanced relative to the P​P𝑃𝑃PP or V​P𝑉𝑃V!P sector.
content_sections.IV Conclusions: In this work, we have shown that the hadronic decays D→a0​​π→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝜋Dto a0pi serve a remarkable place for discriminating the internal structure of the a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 meson. If a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 is a q​q¯𝑞¯𝑞qbarq state, the predicted branching fractions of D→a0​+​π→𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript980𝜋Dto a0+pi are too small by one to two orders of magnitude compared to the experiment as the corresponding amplitude is suppressed by the smallness of the a0+superscriptsubscript𝑎0a0+ decay constant, while the predicted rates for D+→a0​0​P→superscript𝐷subscript𝑎0superscript9800𝑃D+to a00P and D0→a0​−,0​P→superscript𝐷0subscript𝑎0superscript9800𝑃D0to a0-,0P are usually too large compared to the data. In particular, the ratios r+⁣/−subscript𝑟absentr+/- and r+⁣/0subscript𝑟absent0r+/0 naïvely expected to be at a few percent level are measured to be around 7.5 and 2.6, respectively. The above difficulties can be resolved in the tetraquark model of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0 as there exist additional T𝑇T-like topological diagrams, namely, T𝑇tildeT and T¯¯𝑇overlineT. Although both amplitudes are nonfactorizable and thus not calculable, their information can be extracted from data. We find T¯≈T¯𝑇𝑇overlineTapprox T and T≈−0.25​T𝑇0.25𝑇tildeTapprox-0.25T. A product of this study shows that the decay Ds+→a0+​π0+a00​π+→superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑠superscriptsubscript𝑎0superscript𝜋0superscriptsubscript𝑎00superscript𝜋Ds+to a0+pi0+a00pi+ is not a purely W𝑊W-annihilation process and it is governed by the tetraquark topology T¯¯𝑇overlineT. Therefore, measurements of D→a0​​P→𝐷subscript𝑎0980𝑃Dto a0P decays lend strong support to the tetraquark picture of a0​subscript𝑎0980a0.

sorted_papers.title:
Searching a systematics for nonfactorizable contribution to B-and B0
  mesons
sorted_papers.abstract:
Two-body weak decays / and are examined under isospin analysis to study
nonfactorizable contributions. After extracting the strong phases and obtaining
the factorizable contributions from spectator-quark diagrams for Nc=3, we
determine nonfactorizable isospin amplitudes from the experimental data for
these modes. Our results support the universality of ratio of nonfactorizable
isospin reduced amplitudes for these decays within experimental errors. In
order to show that this systematics is not coincidental, we also plot our
results w. r. t. this ratio.

sorted_papers.title:
Analysis of Hadronic Weak Decays of Charmed Baryons in the Topological
  Diagrammatic Approach
sorted_papers.abstract:
We perform a global fit to the experimental data of two-body charmed baryon
decays based on the topological diagrammatic approach (TDA) and take into
account the phase shifts between $S$- and $P$-wave amplitudes as inspired by
the recent BESIII measurement of the decay asymmetry in the decay
$\Lambda_c^+\to \Xi^0K^+$. The TDA has the advantage that it is more intuitive,
graphic and easier to implement model calculations. The measured branching
fractions and decay asymmetries are well accommodated in the TDA except for a
few modes, in particular, the predicted ${\cal B}(\Xi_c^0\to
\Xi^-\pi^+)=(2.83\pm0.10)\%$ is larger than its current value. The equivalence
of the TDA and the irreducible SU(3) approach (IRA) is established. We show
that the number of the minimum set of tensor invariants in the IRA and the
topological amplitudes in the TDA is the same and present their relations. The
predicted magnitudes of $S$- and $P$-wave amplitudes and their phase shifts are
presented for measured and yet-to-be-measured modes in both the TDA and IRA
which can be tested in the near future. Besides the decay $\Lambda_c^+\to \Xi^0
K^+$, there exist several modes which proceed only through $W$-exchange. In
particular, the observed channel $\Xi_c^0\to \Sigma^+ K^-$ should have phase
shifts similar to that in $\Lambda_c^+\to \Xi^0 K^+$ and its decay asymmetry is
predicted to be $-0.21\pm0.17$ which can be used to test our theoretical
framework. In contrast, the TDA leads to a large $\alpha$ of order $-0.93$ for
the decay $\Xi_c^+\to \Xi^0\pi^+$ even after the phase-shift effect is
incorporated in the fit.

sections.4: Kohara, “Quark diagram analysis of charmed baryon decays,” Phys
sections.15: Kohara, “Quark diagram analysis of charmed baryon decays,” Phys
sections.17: Kohara, “Comments on ’Analysis of two-body decays of charmed baryons using the quark diagram scheme’,” [arXiv:hep-ph/9701287 [hep-ph]].

qualitative: The topological diagrammatic approach (TDA) provides an intuitive, graphical, and implementable framework for analyzing two-body charmed baryon weak decays, offering advantages over traditional model-independent approaches.

The TDA incorporates phase shifts between S- and P-wave amplitudes, inspired by recent BESIII measurements of decay asymmetry in $\Lambda_c^+ \to \Xi^0 K^+$, which improves the description of experimental data.

The TDA and the irreducible SU(3) approach (IRA) are equivalent in their underlying structure, with the same minimum number of tensor invariants in IRA matching the number of topological amplitudes in TDA, and explicit relations between them are established.
Several decay modes proceed exclusively through W-exchange diagrams, making them sensitive probes of non-factorizable contributions and offering unique tests of theoretical frameworks.

The decay $\Xi_c^0 \to \Sigma^+ K^-$ is predicted to have a decay asymmetry similar to that of $\Lambda_c^+ \to \Xi^0 K^+$, with a predicted asymmetry of $-0.21 \pm 0.17$, providing a key test of the theoretical model.
The TDA predicts a large decay asymmetry $\alpha \approx -0.93$ for $\Xi_c^+ \to \Xi^0 \pi^+$, even after including phase-shift effects, indicating a potential tension with future measurements.

The TDA successfully describes most measured branching fractions and decay asymmetries, except for $\Xi_c^0 \to \Xi^- \pi^+$, where the predicted branching fraction $(2.83 \pm 0.10)\%$ exceeds the current experimental value.
quantitative: Predicted branching fraction: $\mathcal{B}(\Xi_c^0 \to \Xi^- \pi^+) = (2.83 \pm 0.10)\%$
Predicted decay asymmetry: $\alpha(\Xi_c^0 \to \Sigma^+ K^-) = -0.21 \pm 0.17$
Predicted decay asymmetry: $\alpha(\Xi_c^+ \to \Xi^0 \pi^+) \approx -0.93$ (after phase-shift inclusion)
Phase shift effects between S- and P-wave amplitudes are incorporated based on BESIII measurement in $\Lambda_c^+ \to \Xi^0 K^+$
The number of minimum tensor invariants in IRA equals the number of topological amplitudes in TDA, establishing their equivalence

content_sections.I Introduction: The progresses in the study of hadronic decays of charmed baryons, both experimentally and theoretically, had been very slow before 2014. Not only most of the experimental measurements were older ones (for a review, see Refs. citebib.bib1, bib.bib2), but also almost all the model calculations of charmed baryon decays were carried out before millennium. Indeed, theoretical interest in hadronic weak decays of charmed baryons peaked around the early 1990s and then faded away. This situation was drastically changed after 2014 as there were several major breakthroughs in charmed-baryon experiments in regard to the weak decays of Λc+superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac+ and Ξc+,0superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0Xic+,0 (for a review, see Ref. citebib.bib3). For example, the absolute branching fraction of Λc+→p​K−​π+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐𝑝superscript𝐾superscript𝜋Lambdac+to pK-pi+, which is a benchmark for nearly all other branching fractions of Λc+superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac+, has been measured by Belle citebib.bib4 and BESIII citebib.bib5 independently with much smaller uncertainties. In 2015 BESIII has measured the absolute branching fractions of Λc+superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac+ for more than a dozen of decay modes directly for the first time citebib.bib5. This is a milestone in the study of hadronic charmed baryon decays. Likewise, Belle has reported the first measurements of the absolute branching fractions of Ξc0→Ξ−​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΞsuperscript𝜋Xic0toXi-pi+, Ξc+→Ξ−​π+​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐superscriptΞsuperscript𝜋superscript𝜋Xic+toXi-pi+pi+ and Ξc+→p​K−​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐𝑝superscript𝐾superscript𝜋Xic+to pK-pi+ for the Ξc+,0superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0Xic+,0 systems citebib.bib6, bib.bib7. Considering the charmed baryon decay ℬc→ℬf+P→subscriptℬ𝑐subscriptℬ𝑓𝑃cal Bctocal Bf+P with P𝑃P being a pseudoscalar meson and ℬcsubscriptℬ𝑐cal Bc, ℬfsubscriptℬ𝑓cal Bf the charmed baryon and final-state baryon, respectively, its general decay amplitude reads M​(ℬc→ℬf+P)=i​u¯f​(A−B​γ5)​uc,𝑀→subscriptℬ𝑐subscriptℬ𝑓𝑃𝑖subscript¯𝑢𝑓𝐴𝐵subscript𝛾5subscript𝑢𝑐displaystyle M(cal Bctocal Bf+P)=ibaruf(A-Bgamma5)uc, where A𝐴A and B𝐵B correspond to the parity-violating S𝑆S-wave and parity-conserving P𝑃P-wave amplitudes, respectively. In general, they receive both factorizable and nonfactorizable contributions A=Afac+Anf,B=Bfac+Bnf. formulae-sequence𝐴superscript𝐴facsuperscript𝐴nf𝐵superscript𝐵facsuperscript𝐵nfA=Armfac+Armnf,qquad B=Brmfac+Brmnf. In the 1990s various approaches were developed to describe the nonfactorizable effects in hadronic decays of the charmed baryons Λc+superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐Lambdac+, Ξc+,0superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0Xic+,0 and Ωc0superscriptsubscriptΩ𝑐0Omegac0. These include the covariant confined quark model, the pole model and current algebra (see Ref. citebib.bib3 for references therein). Besides the dynamical model calculations, a very promising approach is to use the approximate SU flavor symmetry of QCD to describe the two-body nonleptonic decays of charmed baryons. There exist two distinct ways in realizing the flavor symmetry, the irreducible SU approach (IRA) and the topological diagram approach (TDA). They provide a powerful tool for a model-independent analysis. Among them, the IRA has become very popular in the past few years. In the IRA, SU tensor invariants are constructed through the short-distance effective Hamiltonian, while in the TDA, the topological diagrams are classified according to the topologies in the flavor flow of weak decay diagrams with all strong-interaction effects included implicitly. Within the framework of the IRA, two-body nonleptonic decays of charmed baryons were first analyzed in Refs. citebib.bib8, bib.bib9 followed by the analysis of Cabibbo-suppressed in Ref. citebib.bib10. After 2014, this approach became rather popular citebib.bib14, bib.bib11, bib.bib12, bib.bib13, bib.bib15, bib.bib17, bib.bib16. However, the early studies of the IRA have overlooked the fact that charmed baryon decays are governed by several different partial-wave amplitudes which have distinct kinematic and dynamic effects. In other words, S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-waves were not distinguished in the early analysis and the IRA amplitudes are fitted only to the measured rates. After the pioneer work in Ref. citebib.bib18, it became a common practice to perform a global fit of both S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave parameters to the data of branching fractions and decay asymmetries citebib.bib21, bib.bib22, bib.bib23, bib.bib19, bib.bib20. Just like the case of hyperon decays, non-trival relative strong phases between S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes may exist, but they were usually not considered in realistic model calculations of the decay asymmetry α𝛼alpha. The first analysis of two-body nonleptonic decays of antitriplet charmed baryons ℬc​(3¯)→ℬ​​M​(8+1)→subscriptℬ𝑐¯3ℬ8𝑀81cal Bc(bar3)tocal BM(8+1) within the framework of the TDA was performed by Kohara citebib.bib24. A subsequent study was given by Chau, Cheng and Tseng (CCT) in Ref. citebib.bib25. Among the recent analyses in the TDA citebib.bib17, bib.bib26, bib.bib27, bib.bib28, 1The TDA analysis in Ref. citebib.bib27 did not assign the appropriate weight factors for the relevant topological diagrams in each decay. there are 19 TDA amplitudes and 7 topological diagrams in Ref. citebib.bib17. Authors of Ref. citebib.bib26 followed the Kohara’s scheme closely with 8 topological diagrams and 8 TDA amplitudes, but did not distinguish between S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave contributions and hence the topological amplitudes were fitted to the branching fractions only. Therefore, unlike the IRA, global fits to the rates and decay asymmetries are still absent in the TDA. Although the TDA has been applied very successfully to charmed meson decays citebib.bib29, bib.bib30, bib.bib31, its application to charmed baryon decays is more complicated than the IRA. As stressed in Ref. citebib.bib17, it is easy to determine the independent amplitudes in the IRA, while the TDA gives some redundancy. Some of the amplitudes are not independent and therefore should be absorbed into other amplitudes. Nevertheless, the TDA has the advantage that it is more intuitive, graphic and easier to implement model calculations. The extracted topological amplitudes by fitting to available data will enable us to probe the relative importance of different underlying decay mechanisms, and to relate one process to another at the topological amplitude level. In this work, we are going to show that the TDA is applicable to charmed baryon decays as well and it has the same number of independent amplitudes as that of the IRA. The Cabibbo-favored mode Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+ which proceeds only through W𝑊W-exchange deserves special attention. Early studies in 1990’s indicated that its S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes are very small due to strong cancellation between various terms (see e. g. Ref. citebib.bib32). For example, the use of current algebra implies a vanishing S𝑆S-wave in the SU limit. Consequently, the calculated branching fraction is too small compared to experiment and the predicted α𝛼alpha is zero owing to the vanishing S𝑆S-wave amplitude. It is thus striking that the approach based on the IRA tends to predict a large decay asymmetry close to unity citebib.bib18, bib.bib19, bib.bib20. This long-standing puzzle was finally resolved by a recent BESIII measurement citebib.bib33. Not only the decay asymmetry αΞ0​K+=0.01±0.16subscript𝛼superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾plus-or-minus0.010.16alphaXi0K+=0.01pm 0.16 was found to be consistent with zero, but also the measured Lee-Yang parameter βΞ0​K+=−0.64±0.69subscript𝛽superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾plus-or-minus0.640.69betaXi0K+=-0.64pm 0.69 was nonzero, implying a phase difference between S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes, δP−δS=−1.55±0.25subscript𝛿𝑃subscript𝛿𝑆plus-or-minus1.550.25deltaP-deltaS.55pm 0.25 or 1.59±0.25plus-or-minus1.590.251.59pm 0.25 rad. Since |cos⁡(δP−δS)|∼0.02similar-tosubscript𝛿𝑃subscript𝛿𝑆0.02|cos(deltaP-deltaS)|sim 0.02, this accounts for the smallness of αΞ0​K+subscript𝛼superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾alphaXi0K+. This first direct evidence supporting the existence of strong phases in the partial-wave amplitudes of hadronic charmed baryon decays plays a pivotal role in a further exploration of C​P𝐶𝑃C!P violation in the charmed baryon sector. Recently, a new analysis of charmed baryon decays based on the IRA that takes into account the phase shifts of the partial-wave amplitudes has been put forward in Ref. citebib.bib34. In this work we shall perform a similar study within the framework of the TDA. Since the TDA has been applied very successfully to charmed meson decays, it is conceivable that the same approach is applicable to the charmed baryon sector. The layout of this work is as follows. In Sec. II we first discuss the choice of octet-baryon wave wave functions. After writing down the general expression of the decay amplitudes in the TDA, we show that the number of independent amplitudes can be reduced through the Körner-Pati-Woo theorem and the removal of redundancy. The equivalence of the TDA and IRA is explicitly demonstrated in Sec. III. Sec. IV is devoted to the numerical analysis and fitting results. Sec. V comes to our conclusions. The relevant experimental results are collected in the Appendix. A short version of this work has been presented in Ref. citebib.bib35.
content_sections.II Formalism: Since baryons are made of three quarks in contrast to two quarks for the mesons, the application of TDA to the baryon case will inevitably lead to some complications. For example, the symmetry of the quarks in flavor space could be different. As stated in the Introduction, there exist two seemingly different analyses of two-body nonleptonic decays of antitriplet charmed baryons within the framework of the TDA: one by Kohara citebib.bib24 and the other by Chau, Cheng and Tseng (CCT) in Ref. citebib.bib25. content_sections.III Equivalence of TDA and IRA: In this section we will consider the general SU-invariant decay amplitudes in the IRA. To demonstrate the equivalence between the TDA and IRA, we need to show that the number of the minimum set of tensor invariants in the IRA and the topological amplitudes in the TDA is the same. If the number of minimum independent amplitudes is different, the global fit to the data will yield different fitting results for branching fractions, decay asymmetries and phase shifts. content_sections.V Conclusion: Inspired by the recent BESIII measurement of the decay asymmetry and the phase shift between S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes in the decay Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+, we have performed a global fit to the experimental data of charmed baryon decays based on the TDA which has the advantage that it is more intuitive, graphic and easier to implement model calculations. Our main results are as follows. • In order to draw the topological diagrams and construct the relevant amplitudes in the TDA, we employ the anti-symmetric matrix (ℬc)i​jsuperscriptsubscriptℬ𝑐𝑖𝑗(cal Bc)ij for the charmed baryon and (B8)i​j​ksubscriptsubscript𝐵8𝑖𝑗𝑘(B8)ijk for the octet baryon, where the indices i,j,k𝑖𝑗𝑘i,j,k stand for light quark flavors. • The wave functions of octet baryons can be represented in several different manners, but physics is independent of the convention on chooses. We use the bases ψ​A12𝜓subscript8subscript𝐴12psiA12 and ψ​S12𝜓subscript8subscript𝑆12psiS12 to assign different topological diagrams and amplitudes. After applying for the KPW theorem, the number of independent topological diagrams and amplitudes is reduced to 7. At this stage, there still exist 2 redundant degrees of freedom through redefinition. We conclude that the minimum set of the topological amplitudes in the TDA is 5. • To demonstrate the equivalence between the TDA and IRA, we have shown that the number of the minimum set of tensor invariants in the IRA and the topological amplitudes in the TDA is the same and presented their relations. • As there are 5 independent tilde TDA amplitudes, we have totally 19 unknown parameters to describe the magnitudes and the phases of the respective S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes. The measured branching fractions and decay asymmetries are well accommodated in the TDA except for three modes: Ξc0→Ξ−​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΞsuperscript𝜋Xic0toXi-pi+, Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+ and the ratio ℛΞ−​K+subscriptℛsuperscriptΞsuperscript𝐾cal RXi-K+. The χ2superscript𝜒2chi2 value is 2.0 per degree of freedom. The predicted ℬ​(Ξc0→Ξ−​π+)=(2.83±0.10)%ℬ→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΞsuperscript𝜋percentplus-or-minus2.830.10cal B(Xic0toXi-pi+)=(2.83pm 0.10)% is larger than its current value, but it is in agood agreement with the sum rule derived in both the TDA and IRA. This needs to be tested in the near future. • The phase difference δP−δSsubscript𝛿𝑃subscript𝛿𝑆deltaP-deltaS between S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes is subject to a sign ambiguity which can be resolved by the measurement of the transverse asymmetry β𝛽beta. Hence, more accurate measurements of β𝛽beta in Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+ and other modes are called for. • When the strong phases δSXisuperscriptsubscript𝛿𝑆subscript𝑋𝑖deltaSXi and δPXisuperscriptsubscript𝛿𝑃subscript𝑋𝑖deltaPXi are turned off for the global fit to the data, the decay asymmetry α𝛼alpha is found to be close to unity in magnitude for some of the modes, e. g. , Λc+→Σ+​η,Ξ0​K+,Σ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΣ𝜂superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾superscriptΣ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toSigma+eta,Xi0K+,Sigma0K+, Ξc+→Ξ0​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝜋Xic+toXi0pi+ and Ξc0→Λ0​KS0,Ξ0​η→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΛ0superscriptsubscript𝐾𝑆0superscriptΞ0𝜂Xic0toLambda0KS0,Xi0eta. The nearly vanishing αΞ0​K+subscript𝛼superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾alphaXi0K+ measured by BESIII implies the necessity of incorporating phase shifts in order to accommodate the data. The measurement of αΞc0→Λ​KSsubscript𝛼→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0Λsubscript𝐾𝑆alphaXic0toLambda KS and αΞc0→Ξ0​ηsubscript𝛼→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΞ0𝜂alphaXic0toXi0eta in the future will also help understand the phase shifts which are predicted to be 1.89±0.25plus-or-minus1.890.251.89pm 0.25 and 1.48±0.24plus-or-minus1.480.241.48pm 0.24 (both in rad), respectively. In contrast, it is also important to measure the decay asymmetry of Ξc+→Ξ0​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝜋Xic+toXi0pi+ to see if its largeness of order −0.930.93-0.93 is not affected even after the phase-shift effect is incorporated. • The fit results for the decay asymmetry and the phase shift δP−δSsubscript𝛿𝑃subscript𝛿𝑆deltaP-deltaS for Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+ are consistent with the BESIII measurements, though our fit of its branching fraction is slightly smaller than the measured one. Nevertheless, our TDA results are very close to those obtained in the IRA. • Besides the decay Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+, there exist several modes Ξc0→Σ+​K−,Σ+​π−,p​K−,p​π−,n​π0→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΣsuperscript𝐾superscriptΣsuperscript𝜋𝑝superscript𝐾𝑝superscript𝜋𝑛superscript𝜋0Xic0toSigma+K-,Sigma+pi-,pK-,ppi-,npi0 and Ξc+→p​π0,n​π+→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐𝑝superscript𝜋0𝑛superscript𝜋Xic+to ppi0,npi+ which proceed only from W𝑊W-exchange characterized by the topological amplitude E1subscript𝐸1tildeE1. By the same token, these modes should have phase shifts similar to that in Λc+→Ξ0​K+→superscriptsubscriptΛ𝑐superscriptΞ0superscript𝐾Lambdac+toXi0K+ and their decay asymmetries are suppressed. In particular, we predict αΞc0→Σ+​K−=−0.21±0.17subscript𝛼→superscriptsubscriptΞ𝑐0superscriptΣsuperscript𝐾plus-or-minus0.210.17alphaXic0toSigma+K-=-0.21pm 0.17 which can be used to test our theoretical framework. • For yet-to-be-measured modes, we have presented the fitting magnitudes of S𝑆S- and P𝑃P-wave amplitudes and their phase shifts in both the TDA and IRA which can be tested in the near future. Acknowledgements. We would like to thank Pei-Rong Li and Chia-Wei Liu for valuable discussions. This research was supported in part by the Ministry of Science and Technology of R. O. C. under Grant No. MOST-112-2112-M-001-026 and the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grant No. U1932104.

sorted_papers.title:
The factorization-assisted topological-amplitude approach and its
  applications
sorted_papers.abstract:
Heavy meson decays provide an important platform for studies of both QCD and
electroweak dynamics, which may contain some portals to understanding of
nonperturbative QCD and physics beyond the Standard Model. The
factorization-assisted topological-amplitude approach was proposed to study
two-body non-leptonic $D$ meson decays, where a promising QCD inspired approach
from first principles is still missing. It was also applied to $B$ meson decays
whose subleading power contributions are difficult to calculate. By factorizing
topological amplitudes into short distance Wilson coefficients and long
distance hadronic matrix elements either to be calculated or to be
parameterized, it provides an effective framework to extract information of
nonperturbative dynamics involved. With important flavor SU(3) breaking effects
taken into account, the data of the decay branching ratios (and also CP
asymmetries in $B$ decays) can be fitted well. The extracted amplitudes were
further applied to make predictions for other observables, such as CP
asymmetries in $D$ decays, mixing parameters in the $D^0-\bar{D}^0$ system, and
so on. By this review, we will describe the formulation of the
factorization-assisted topological-amplitude approach and summarize its
applications in $D$ and $B$ meson decays and highlight some of its
achievements.

sorted_papers.title:
Non-factorisable Contributions of Strong-Penguin Operators in $\Lambda_b
  \to \Lambda \ell^+\ell^-$ Decays
sorted_papers.abstract:
We investigate for the first time a certain class of non-factorisable
contributions of the four-quark operators ${\cal O}_{3-6}$ in the weak
effective Hamiltonian to the $\Lambda_b \to \Lambda \ell^+\ell^-$ decay
amplitude. We focus on the case where a virtual photon is radiated from one of
the light constituents of the $\Lambda_b$ baryon, in the kinematic situation of
large hadronic recoil with an energetic $\Lambda$ baryon in the final state.
The effect on the suitably defined ``non-local form factors'' is calculated
using the light-cone sum rule approach for a correlator with an interpolating
current for the light $\Lambda$ baryon. We find that this approach requires the
introduction of new soft functions that generalise the standard light-cone
distribution amplitudes (LCDAs) for the heavy $\Lambda_b$ baryon. We give a
heuristic discussion of their properties and a model that relates them to the
standard LCDAs. Within this framework, we provide numerical results for the
size of the non-local form factors considered.

sorted_papers.title:
CP violation in quasi-two-body $D\to VP$ decays and three-body $D$
  decays mediated by vector resonances
sorted_papers.abstract:
We re-examine direct CP violation in the quasi-two-body $D\to V\!P$ decays
and study CP asymmetries in three-body $D$ decays proceeding through
intermediate vector resonances within the framework of the topological
amplitude approach for tree amplitudes and the QCD factorization approach for
penguin amplitudes. Using the same mechanism of incorporating long-distance
penguin-exchange amplitude induced from final-state rescattering that nicely
accounts for the CP asymmetry difference between $D^0\to K^+K^-$ and
$\pi^+\pi^-$ modes, we find that CP asymmetry can also occur at the per mille
level in many of the $D\to V\!P$ channels or otherwise be negligibly small. We
point out six golden modes which have sufficiently large branching fractions
and direct CP violation of order $10^{-3}$. In particular, the direct CP
asymmetry difference $\Delta a_{CP}^{V\!P}$ between $D^0\to K^+K^{*-}$ and
$\pi^+\rho^-$ is predicted to be $(-1.61\pm0.33)\times 10^{-3}$, very similar
to the counterpart in the $P\!P$ sector. Besides, we find a positive and large
CP asymmetry of order $10^{-3}$ in $D^0\to K_S K^{*0}$, an example of the
asymmetry induced at the tree level. We take into account the flavor-singlet
QCD-penguin contributions to calculate their effects on the modes such as
$D\to\pi\phi$ and $D\to \eta \phi$. We compare our results with the other
approach in detail. For three-body $D$ decays, we perform the Dalitz plot
analysis of those decays mediated by several vector resonances with the same
mass but different electric charges. Local CP asymmetry varies in magnitude and
sign from region to region and can reach the percent level in certain local
regions of phase space due to interference.

sorted_papers.title: Topological Diagrams and Hadronic Weak Decays of Charmed Baryons
sorted_papers.abstract:
Inspired by the recent BESIII measurement of the decay asymmetry and the
phase shift between $S$- and $P$-wave amplitudes in the decay $\Lambda_c^+\to
\Xi^0K^+$, we perform a global fit to the experimental data of charmed baryon
decays based on the topological diagrammatic approach (TDA) which has the
advantage that it is more intuitive and easier to implement model calculations.
The measured branching fractions and decay asymmetries are well accommodated in
TDA except for three modes, in particular, the predicted ${\cal B}(\Xi_c^0\to
\Xi^-\pi^+)=(2.83\pm0.10)\%$ is larger than its current value. The predicted
magnitudes of $S$- and $P$-wave amplitudes and their phase shifts are presented
for measured and yet-measured modes which can be tested in forthcoming
experiments.

约束项检查：

sorted_papers.title: Two-body Baryonic $B_{u,d,s}$ and $B_c$ to Charmless Final State Decays
sorted_papers.abstract:
We study the rates and direct CP violations of two-body baryonic $\bar
B_{u,d,s}\to {{\rm\bf B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$ and $B^-_c\to{{\rm\bf
B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$ decays, where the final state baryons include
low-lying octet and decuplet baryons. We incorporate topological amplitude
formalism and the factorization approach. Asymptotic relations at large $m_b$
are used to simplify decay amplitudes. Using the most up-to-date data on $\bar
B^0\to p\bar p$ and $B^-\to\Lambda\bar p$ decay rates as inputs, rates and
direct CP violations of $\bar B_{u,d,s}\to {{\rm\bf B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$
decays are revised and predicted. It is interesting that the results on rates
satisfy all existing experimental bounds and some are close to the bounds.
Factorization diagrams contribute to penguin-exchange, exchange, annihilation
and penguin-annihilation amplitudes. Although the resulting penguin-exchange
factorization amplitudes are sizable, the rest suffer from severe chiral
suppression and are sensitive to non-factorizable contributions. The $\bar
B_s\to p\bar p$ decay rate predicted in factorization calculation is very rare,
but it can be enhanced by including non-factorizable contributions. The case
where the rate is enhanced to saturate the present experimental bound is
discussed. As annihilation modes $B^-_c\to{{\rm\bf B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$
decays from factorization calculation are found to be very rare, but they can
be enhanced by including non-factorizable contributions as well. Small direct
CP violations of pure penguin modes in $\Delta S=-1$ $\bar B_{u,d,s}\to{{\rm\bf
B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$ decays are robust predictions of the SM, while
vanishing direct CP violations of exchange modes in $\bar B_{u,d,s}\to{{\rm\bf
B}\overline{\rm\bf B}}'$ decays and in all $B^-_c\to{{\rm\bf B}\overline{\rm\bf
B}}'$ decay modes are null tests of the SM.

sorted_papers.title: Towards a Global Analysis of the $b\to c\bar{u} q$ Puzzle
sorted_papers.abstract: We study the nonleptonic decays $\bar{B}_s^0 \to D_s^{(*)+} \pi^-$ and $\bar{B}^0 \to D^{(*)+} K^-$ within the Weak Effective Theory (WET) up to mass-dimension six. We revisit the calculation of the hadronic matrix elements within QCD Factorization including the full set of WET operators. We recalculate the two-particle contributions to the hard-scattering kernels at next-to-leading order in $\alpha_s$, confirming recent results in the literature. We also calculate the three-particle contributions at leading order in $\alpha_s$, clarifying the procedure, refining the SM results in the literature, and providing for the first time the complete set of contributions within the WET. We use these results to perform a global phenomenological study of the effective couplings, putting bounds on the size of the WET Wilson coefficients in four distinct fit models. The fits include constraints from the nonleptonic $B$-meson decay width, which we calculate at the leading order for the full set of WET operators for the first time. This study is the first one to account for simultaneous variation of up to six effective couplings. We identify two distinct modes in all fit models and discuss how future measurements can be used to distinguish between them.

all_papers.title:
Updated analysis of $D\to PP, V\!P$ and $VV$ decays: Implications for
  $K_S^0-K_L^0$ asymmetries and $D^0$-$\overline {D}^0$ mixing
all_papers.abstract:
An updated analysis of the two-body $D\to PP, V\!P$ and $VV$ decays within
the framework of the topological diagram approach is performed. A global fit to
the Cabibbo-favored (CF) modes in the $V\!P$ sector gives many solutions with
similarly small local minima in $\chi^2$. The solution degeneracy is lifted
once we use them to predict for the singly Cabibbo-suppressed (SCS) modes.
Topological amplitudes are extracted for the $\eta-\eta'$ mixing angles
$\phi=40.4^\circ$ and $43.5^\circ$. The $K_S^0-K_L^0$ asymmetries in $D\to
K_{S,L}^0M$ decays denoted by $R(D,M)$ are studied. While the predicted
$R(D^0,P)$ for $P=\pi^0, \eta$ and $\eta'$ agree with experiment, the
calculated $R(D^+,\pi^+)$, $R(D_s^+, K^+)$, $R(D^0,\omega)$ and $R(D^0,\phi)$
deviate from the data. We conjecture that the relative phase between the
topological amplitudes $(C+A)$ and $(T+C)$ should be slightly smaller than
$90^\circ$ in order to explain the first two discrepancies and that additional
singlet contributions due to the SU(3)-singlet nature of $\omega$ and $\phi$
are needed to account for the last two. For doubly Cabibbo-suppressed (DCS)
$D\to V\!P$ decays, their topological amplitudes (double-primed) cannot be all
the same as the corresponding ones in the CF modes. The assumption of
$E_{V,P}''=E_{V,P}$ for the $W$-exchange amplitude leads to some
inconsistencies with the experiment. Through the measured relative phases
between CF and DCS channels, the relations of $E_{V,P}''$ with $E_{V,P}$ are
determined. Long-distance contributions to the $D^0$-$\overline {D}^0$ mixing
parameter $y$ are evaluated in the exclusive approach. In particular, we focus
on $D\to PP$ and $V\!P$ decays where $y$ can be reliably estimated. We conclude
that $y_{_{P\!P}}\sim (0.110\pm 0.011)\%$ and the lower bound on $y_{_{V\!P}}$
is $(0.220\pm 0.071)\%$.

sections.18: Ambrosino et al., “A global fit to determine the pseudoscalar mixing angle and the gluonium content of the η′superscript𝜂′\eta^{\prime} meson,” JHEP 0907, 105 (2009) [arXiv:0906.3819 [hep-ph]].
